source: Claude session audit (2026-05-29) — privacy guard run before commit
title: Privacy publish guard false-positives on api_key=VARIABLE references — blocks legitimate commits
what_failed: The credential-pattern check (Section 3) flags `api_key=CEREBRAS_API_KEY` in SOLUTIONS.jsonl:48 (S-053) and docs/adr/003:61 as credential leaks and FAILs the whole guard. Both are variable references inside code snippets — the literal key lives in the gitignored app/config.py. The over-match means every commit that touches those already-tracked files trips a FAIL, training the user to ignore the guard (cry-wolf) — which defeats a privacy gate.
where_failed: scripts/passive/privacy_publish_guard.py credential-assignment regex — matches `api_key=` followed by any token, including UPPER_CASE identifiers and other variable names.
why_likely: The regex was written to catch `api_key="sk-..."` literals but doesn't distinguish a literal/quoted secret from a variable reference. No allowance for ALL_CAPS env-var names or unquoted identifiers.
migration_plan: 1. Tighten the credential regex: only flag api_key/secret/token assignments whose RHS is a quoted string literal OR matches a known key shape (sk-, AKIA, ghp_, etc.).
2. Explicitly do NOT flag RHS that is an ALL_CAPS identifier or a bare variable name (api_key=CEREBRAS_API_KEY, token=cfg.token).
3. Add unit tests: api_key="sk-abc123" → FLAG; api_key=CEREBRAS_API_KEY → PASS; token=os.environ['X'] → PASS.
4. Re-run on the current tree; Section 3 should go green without weakening real-secret detection.
implementation_notes: A privacy gate that cries wolf is worse than no gate — it gets routinely overridden. The guard's OTHER sections (private impl tracked, data/logs tracked, tracked-files-match-gitignore) are valuable and correctly PASS; only the credential regex is too loose. Note: this script is itself under the gitignored scripts/ tree, so the fix won't be version-controlled until T-155 is addressed.
analysis: PERSPECTIVE: This bit us immediately — the guard blocked this very session's commit on two variable references. Either the gate is trustworthy or it's theater; a false-positive FAIL on every commit makes it theater. Tighten the regex so a FAIL always means a real secret. Cheap, high-trust-value fix.
